intro: The clearest difference between Void and Cody is pricing — Void is free, while Cody is freemium (has a free tier). Beyond that, lock-in — Void has low lock-in (OSS VS Code fork), whereas Cody has low lock-in (Sourcegraph AI, OSS core). Void fits privacy-conscious developers who want to bring their own LLM keys and avoid SaaS middlemen; Cody fits accelerating coding workflow. On the strengths side, Void is cited for open-source Cursor alternative with no middleman backend, while Cody is cited for accelerating coding workflow. The honest trade-off: Void is the wrong call for users wanting the most polished agent experience or built-in team features; Cody is the wrong call for non-coding roles; Void users flag features lag Cursor and Windsurf by a meaningful margin, and Cody users flag non-coding roles. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap. On developer experience the data shows Void at 4/5 and Cody at 5/5, with transparency at 5/5 and 4/5 respectively.
quick_take: Void is for privacy-conscious developers who want to bring; Cody is for accelerating coding workflow; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Void if your use case is privacy-conscious developers who want to bring their own LLM keys and avoid SaaS middlemen, and a free pricing shape fits your budget better than freemium (has a free tier).
verdict_b: Choose Cody if your use case is accelerating coding workflow, and a freemium (has a free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than free.

faq.a: Void is free, while Cody uses a freemium model, so on listed pricing alone Void comes out cheaper. That said, total cost depends on operational effort, hosting, and whether you need features that Cody's paid tiers cover. Compare the pricing pages directly for current numbers.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Void to Cody?
faq.a: Migrating from Void to Cody is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Void has low lock-in, so exporting state is typically straightforward. Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

On our developer-experience score, Cody is rated 5/5 and Void 4/5, so Cody edges ahead. Scores like this smooth over real differences in docs, CLI feel, and SDK quality, so treat them as a starting point. Spend an afternoon in each before relying on the number.

faq.q: Is Cody a good alternative to Void?
faq.a: Cody is a reasonable alternative to Void when Cody fits accelerating coding workflow. If your use case matches Void's sweet spot — privacy-conscious developers who want to bring their own LLM keys and avoid SaaS middlemen — staying with Void is probably safer. Void itself is not meant for users wanting the most polished agent experience or built-in team features, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
